mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 2.0% signals monetary accommodation that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors rotate into equities seeking yield.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS falls below 2.0% and remains there for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following trigger activation are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month payroll employment growth decelerates below 100,000 net new jobs, signaling weakening labor demand that precedes equity market weakness as investors anticipate reduced corporate earnings from lower consumer spending.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change falls below 100,000 for 2 consecutive months
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market softening
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC over 60-day horizon is negative and statistically significant (p < 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales growth (RSAFS) combined with housing starts (HOUST) both accelerating simultaneously indicates robust consumer and residential demand, which drives corporate earnings expectations upward and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: mid-cycle expansion with synchronized demand growth
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction term (RSAFS × HOUST momentum) falls below 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns show no significant positive skew when both series are in top quartile (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month while inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates, indicating stagflationary pressure that compresses profit margins and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month change is negative AND CPIAUCSL month-over-month change exceeds 0.5%
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances when both trigger conditions are met, or Rank IC of the interaction term is positive and statistically significant (p < 0.05) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month period, signaling deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger equity market repricing downward as investors anticipate reduced consumer spending and corporate earnings.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3pp over 3 consecutive months
regime_hint: post-peak labor market with rising unemployment
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns following trigger activation are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) accelerates above 3.0% annualized rate while housing starts (HOUST) remain above 1.2 million units annualized, indicating robust economic expansion that supports equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth exceeds 3.0% AND HOUST remains above 1.2 million units
regime_hint: strong cyclical expansion with broad-based demand
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10) when both conditions are met, or Rank IC of the joint signal falls below 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) decelerates below 2.5% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains above 3.0%, indicating the Fed is in restrictive territory relative to inflation dynamics, which constrains equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth falls below 2.5% AND FEDFUNDS remains above 3.0%
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 58% of triggered instances, or Rank IC of the interaction term is positive and statistically significant (p < 0.05) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) accelerates month-over-month above 0.5% while inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains stable below 3.0%, indicating healthy supply-side growth without inflationary pressure, which supports profit margins and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month change exceeds 0.5% AND CPIAUCSL month-over-month change is below 0.3%
regime_hint: goldilocks regime with supply growth and price stability
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns following trigger activation are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10).
